question: Will CPI rise more than 1.3% in March 2026?
reasoning: The official March 2026 CPI data was released today showing a 0.3% monthly increase, which is significantly below the 1.3% threshold required for YES resolution. Since the actual result (0.3%) is definitively lower than the market threshold (1.3%), the probability of YES resolution is 0%. The market appears to have not yet incorporated this official data release.
key_factors: Official BLS data released showing 0.3% March CPI increase
Actual result is 1.0 percentage point below threshold
Data is final and official from Bureau of Labor Statistics
risks: Potential data revision (though historically rare for monthly CPI)
Market may not have updated to reflect morning data release
No meaningful upside risk given definitively resolved outcome
